title: Spicy Broiled Irish Carrot Stew
description: A hearty, one-pot Irish-inspired stew featuring sweet carrots, smoky spices, and a burst of heat, finished with a quick broil for caramelized edges.

ingredients.name: dried thyme
ingredients.notes: fresh or dried, whichever is available

instructions: Preheat the oven to its highest broil setting, placing a rack about 4 inches from the heat source.

Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large, oven‑safe pot over medium‑high heat. Add the sliced onions and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable stock, then stir in the smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the sliced carrots to the pot, reduce the heat to medium, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until the carrots are tender but still hold their shape.
Stir in the chopped kale and cook for an additional 5 minutes until the greens wilt. If desired, stir in lemon juice for a touch of acidity.

Transfer the stew to a shallow baking dish or spread it evenly in the pot if it’s oven‑safe. Place under the broiler for 3–5 minutes, watching closely, until the top is golden and slightly caramelized. Remove from the oven and serve hot.
